title: Extract Cloud & SDK to Sibling Repos + Fix 5 Cloud Issues
overview:
Extract src/cloud/ (15 files) to ../browse_cloud and src/sdk/ (6 files) to ../browse_sdk as independent repos. browse_cloud depends on @ulpi/browse as a PUBLISHED npm package via built library output -- not raw .ts files. This requires adding a library build target (dist/lib.mjs) to browse_cli alongside the existing CLI bundle (dist/browse.cjs), plus a types pipeline (tsc --emitDeclarationOnly) producing dist/types/*.d.ts, plus an 'exports' field in package.json with 'types' conditions. browse_sdk has zero CLI deps and extracts cleanly. src/sdk.ts (local SDK using BrowserManager directly) stays in browse_cli.

Fixes 5 of 7 cloud issues. VM networking and WS streaming for isolated sessions are out of scope.
scopeChallenge:
The critical constraint is the REAL package boundary: browse_cloud must import from @ulpi/browse as a built npm package, not raw TypeScript source. This means browse_cli needs TWO build outputs plus a types pipeline:

1. dist/browse.cjs -- the CLI entry point (already exists)
2. dist/lib.mjs -- library entry point exporting the ~25 symbols cloud needs
3. dist/types/lib.d.ts -- TypeScript declarations generated by tsc --emitDeclarationOnly

The package.json 'exports' field must have 'types' conditions pointing to dist/types/lib.d.ts and 'import' conditions pointing to dist/lib.mjs. Without this, 'import { SessionManager } from "@ulpi/browse"' won't resolve both runtime AND types in a consumer project.

tsconfig.json already has declaration: true, declarationMap: true, outDir: dist, rootDir: src. The build:lib script must run BOTH esbuild (for dist/lib.mjs) AND tsc --emitDeclarationOnly --declaration --outDir dist/types (for .d.ts files).

Cloud tests import 4 CLI modules (commands/write, session/manager, session/persist, session/target-factory). These must be added to src/lib.ts exports, and tests rewritten to import from '@ulpi/browse' -- not relative paths. This is a boundary rewrite, not a directory move.

The 5 in-scope cloud bugs:
- BLOCK 1: Direct-mode resume doesn't restore session state (server.ts:474)
- BLOCK 2: Direct-mode freeze drops allowedDomains (server.ts:440 -- hardcoded null)
- CONCERN 4: VM resume handle doesn't set allowedDomains (vm-orchestrator.ts:391)
- CONCERN 5: Per-key maxConcurrent never enforced in command dispatch
- CONCERN 7: VM lifecycle test placeholders (cloud-microvm.test.ts:641,657)

Out of scope:
- BLOCK 3: VM networking requires external host-side tap/NAT/DHCP setup (structural gap, not a code fix)
- CONCERN 6: Isolated-session WS streaming requires WS proxy to container internal server

prerequisites.assumption: esbuild supports multiple entry points for separate CLI and library builds
prerequisites.verification: esbuild docs confirm multiple --entry-points or separate build invocations

prerequisites.assumption: tsconfig.json already has declaration: true, declarationMap: true, outDir: dist, rootDir: src
Node.js 'exports' field supports 'types' condition for TypeScript resolution
prerequisites.verification: Node.js docs + TypeScript 5.0+ moduleResolution: bundler supports exports.types

prerequisites.assumption: Cloud imports are limited to 7 specific modules with ~25 symbols
prerequisites.verification: grep -r "from '../" src/cloud/ shows exactly 8 import lines across server.ts, sessions.ts, and ws.ts

prerequisites.assumption: SDK has zero CLI dependencies -- only internal imports
prerequisites.verification: grep -r "from '../" src/sdk/ shows zero matches (SDK is self-contained)

prerequisites.assumption: No src/ file in browse_cli imports from cloud/ or sdk/
prerequisites.verification: grep shows zero matches for cloud/ or sdk/ imports in src/ (excluding comments)

prerequisites.assumption: freezeSession in session/persist.ts reads domainFilter.domains from the session object
resumeSession in session/persist.ts creates a fresh target and restores tabs/cookies/state

nonGoals: Publishing browse_cloud or browse_sdk to npm (local file: deps for now)
Monorepo workspace setup (pnpm/npm workspaces) -- three independent repos
Cloud server HTTP integration tests (existing tests are module-level)
VM networking fix -- requires host-side tap/NAT/DHCP setup, not a code change (BLOCK 3)
WebSocket streaming for isolated sessions -- requires WS proxy to container internal server (CONCERN 6)
Automated CI/CD pipeline for the new repos (future work)
Refactoring cloud server.ts into smaller modules

contracts.producer: dist/lib.mjs (runtime) + dist/types/lib.d.ts (types) via src/lib.ts entry point
contracts.shape: Runtime: SessionManager, Session(type), RecordedStep(type), createBrowserTargetFactory, CreatedTarget(type), SessionTargetFactory(type), TargetCreateOptions(type), executeCommand, ExecuteOptions(type), ExecuteResult(type), registry, ensureDefinitionsRegistered, getRuntime, BrowserRuntime(type), SessionBuffers, LogEntry(type), NetworkEntry(type), saveSessionState, loadSessionState, hasPersistedState, freezeSession, hasFrozenManifest, loadFrozenManifest, FrozenSessionManifest(type), DEFAULTS, CLOUD_DEFAULTS. Tests only: handleWriteCommand.
contracts.consistencyRule: Cloud imports ONLY from '@ulpi/browse' (the built package). NEVER from raw .ts files. If browse_cli adds/removes/renames an export, cloud build fails immediately.

contracts.shape: BrowseClient, BrowseSession, Transport(interface), LocalTransport, CloudTransport, CloudApiError
contracts.consistencyRule: browse_sdk has ZERO dependencies on @ulpi/browse. Transport interface is self-contained.

contracts.shape: NEVER: browse_cli must never import from browse_cloud
contracts.consistencyRule: Verified by grep. browse_cloud depends on browse_cli, not the reverse.

failureModes.risk: Library build output (dist/lib.mjs) missing exports that cloud needs -- browse_cloud TypeScript compilation fails
failureModes.mitigation: src/lib.ts explicitly re-exports every symbol cloud imports. Build script produces dist/lib.mjs via esbuild with --format=esm. Verified by tsc --noEmit in browse_cloud after linking.

failureModes.risk: Types pipeline produces .d.ts files but package.json exports.types doesn't point to them correctly -- consumer project can't resolve types
failureModes.mitigation: TASK-001 acceptance criteria requires: 'import { SessionManager } from "@ulpi/browse"' resolves both runtime AND types in a consumer project. Verified by tsc --noEmit in a test consumer.

failureModes.risk: package.json 'exports' field breaks the existing CLI bin entry point
failureModes.mitigation: The '.' export uses 'import' and 'default' conditions (ESM-only library). bin field is separate from exports and still points to dist/browse.cjs for CLI usage. Test by running 'node dist/browse.cjs --help' after build.
Add guard to scripts/check-legacy-imports.mjs. browse_cli never imports from browse_cloud.

failureModes.risk: Direct-mode resume provisions a session but doesn't restore tabs/cookies -- user loses page state
failureModes.mitigation: Call resumeSession() from session/persist.ts. resumeSession handles tabs, cookies, active tab restoration with graceful fallbacks.

failureModes.risk: handleWriteCommand import in cloud.test.ts breaks after extraction -- it's a deep CLI internal
failureModes.mitigation: Export handleWriteCommand from src/lib.ts (it's needed for cloud test's goto). This is a test-only export.

failureModes.risk: VM networking fails silently when no networkProvider -- VMs get unreachable IPs
failureModes.mitigation: Guard that THROWS (not warns) when VM mode is enabled without networkProvider. This is a 5-line change.

testCoverageMap.codepath: Library build produces dist/lib.mjs with all exports + dist/types/lib.d.ts with declarations
package.json exports resolves both runtime AND types for 'import { SessionManager } from "@ulpi/browse"'
CLI bin entry still works after exports field added
SDK builds and tests independently (zero CLI deps)
Cloud imports resolve to @ulpi/browse package (not relative paths)
Cloud tests compile and run against @ulpi/browse package imports (boundary tests)
Direct-mode resume calls resumeSession to restore tabs/cookies/state
VM resume handle carries allowedDomains + VM mode throws without networkProvider
Per-key maxConcurrent enforced in command dispatch
WS streaming limitation documented, VM test placeholders cleaned up
All three repos compile and test independently

tasks.title: Library entry point + types pipeline + exports in browse_cli
acceptanceCriteria: 'npm run build' produces dist/browse.cjs (CLI), dist/lib.mjs (library), and dist/types/lib.d.ts (declarations) without errors. Failure case: tsc --emitDeclarationOnly fails because lib.ts imports a module with type errors.

'node dist/browse.cjs --help' still works -- CLI entry point not broken by exports field. Failure case: Node.js resolves the exports '.' condition to lib.mjs instead of browse.cjs when invoked as a bin.

'import { SessionManager } from "@ulpi/browse"' resolves both runtime AND types in a consumer project with moduleResolution: bundler. Verified by: tsc --noEmit in a consumer that imports SessionManager and uses it as a type. Failure case: types condition missing or pointing to wrong path, consumer gets 'any' type.

tasks.title: Scaffold browse_sdk -- move src/sdk/ + verify src/sdk.ts stays
tasks.description:
Create /Users/ciprian/work_cip/browse/browse_sdk/ as a standalone package. Move 6 SDK files from src/sdk/ and 1 test file. SDK uses only Node.js built-ins (http, fs, path). Transport interface is self-contained in session.ts.

**Directory structure:**
```
browse_sdk/
  package.json       (@ulpi/browse-sdk, type: module)
  tsconfig.json      (strict, ESNext, bundler resolution)
  src/
    index.ts        (barrel -- from browse_cli/src/sdk/index.ts)
    client.ts       (from browse_cli/src/sdk/client.ts)
    session.ts      (from browse_cli/src/sdk/session.ts)
    transports/
      index.ts    (from browse_cli/src/sdk/transports/index.ts)
      local.ts    (from browse_cli/src/sdk/transports/local.ts)
      cloud.ts    (from browse_cli/src/sdk/transports/cloud.ts)
  test/
    sdk.test.ts     (from browse_cli/test/cloud-sdk.test.ts, renamed)
```

**package.json** -- name: @ulpi/browse-sdk, no @ulpi/browse dependency. Dependencies: none. DevDependencies: typescript, vitest.

**Import updates in test/sdk.test.ts** -- change from '../src/sdk/session' to '../src/session', from '../src/sdk/client' to '../src/client', etc.

**Critical verification:** src/sdk.ts (the local SDK that uses BrowserManager directly) must NOT be touched. It stays in browse_cli. Only src/sdk/ (the directory) is moved.

**Verify:** `npx tsc --noEmit` passes in browse_sdk/. `grep -r '@ulpi/browse' browse_sdk/src/` returns zero matches.
acceptanceCriteria: browse_sdk/ exists at /Users/ciprian/work_cip/browse/browse_sdk/ with package.json (name: @ulpi/browse-sdk), tsconfig.json, and src/ containing all 6 SDK files. Failure case: directory created but missing transports/ subdirectory.
'npx tsc --noEmit' passes in browse_sdk/ with zero errors. Failure case: session.ts imports a type from @ulpi/browse that doesn't exist in the self-contained Transport interface.

'grep -r "@ulpi/browse" browse_sdk/src/' returns zero matches -- SDK has zero CLI deps. 'import { createBrowser } from "@ulpi/browse"' in browse_cli/src/sdk.ts still works. Failure case: src/sdk.ts accidentally deleted during move.

tasks.title: Scaffold browse_cloud repo -- move 15 files, rewrite imports to @ulpi/browse
tasks.description:
Create /Users/ciprian/work_cip/browse/browse_cloud/ with all 15 cloud source files. Update ALL imports from relative CLI paths to @ulpi/browse package imports. Internal cloud imports stay relative.

**Directory structure:**
```
browse_cloud/
  package.json       (@ulpi/browse-cloud, depends on @ulpi/browse)
  tsconfig.json      (strict, ESNext, bundler resolution)
  src/
    index.ts, server.ts, auth.ts, sessions.ts, ws.ts,
    docker.ts, orchestrator.ts, orchestrator-interface.ts,
    proxy.ts, reaper.ts, warm-pool.ts,
    firecracker.ts, vm-orchestrator.ts, vm-warm-pool.ts,
    golden-snapshot.ts
```

**Import rewrite mapping (all become 'from "@ulpi/browse"'):**
- src/cloud/server.ts line 15: `from '../engine/resolver'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/server.ts line 16: `from '../session/manager'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/server.ts line 17: `from '../session/target-factory'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/server.ts line 18: `from '../automation/executor'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/server.ts line 19: `from '../automation/registry'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/server.ts line 27: `from '../constants'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/sessions.ts line 14: `from '../session/manager'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`
- src/cloud/ws.ts line 20: `from '../network/buffers'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'`

**Internal cloud imports stay relative** -- e.g. `from './auth'`, `from './sessions'` remain unchanged.

**package.json** -- name: @ulpi/browse-cloud, dependency: `"@ulpi/browse": "file:../browse_cli"`. DevDependencies: typescript, vitest, playwright (for type imports).

**tsconfig.json** -- NO paths alias. TypeScript resolves @ulpi/browse through the file: dependency in node_modules, which points to browse_cli's package.json exports (dist/types/lib.d.ts). This ensures tsc validates against the BUILT types, not source. If dist/types is stale or missing, tsc fails — which is the correct signal.

**Verify (two-step, both required):**
1. `cd ../browse_cli && npm run build` -- ensures dist/lib.mjs and dist/types/lib.d.ts are fresh
2. `cd ../browse_cloud && npx tsc --noEmit` -- resolves @ulpi/browse via node_modules -> file: link -> package.json exports -> dist/types/lib.d.ts. This MUST NOT use tsconfig paths.
3. `grep -r "from '\.\./'" browse_cloud/src/` returns zero matches.
acceptanceCriteria: browse_cloud/ exists with all 15 source files and package.json depending on @ulpi/browse. Failure case: package.json dependency points to wrong path or uses 'workspace:*' instead of 'file:../browse_cli'.
'grep -r "from \'\.\./'" browse_cloud/src/' returns zero matches -- no relative imports to CLI internals. Failure case: one import missed during rewrite, still points to ../session/manager.

'npx tsc --noEmit' passes in browse_cloud/ -- all @ulpi/browse imports resolve correctly for both runtime and type symbols. Failure case: a type-only import (e.g. 'type Session') not exported from lib.ts.

tasks.title: Rewrite cloud tests as boundary tests in browse_cloud
tasks.description:
This is a BOUNDARY REWRITE, not a directory move. Cloud tests import 4 CLI modules (commands/write, session/manager, session/persist, session/target-factory). These must be rewritten to import from '@ulpi/browse' package, not relative paths. A test in browse_cloud must actually compile and run against the linked package.

**Files to move (4 test files):**
- test/cloud.test.ts -> browse_cloud/test/cloud.test.ts
- test/cloud-containers.test.ts -> browse_cloud/test/cloud-containers.test.ts
- test/cloud-microvm.test.ts -> browse_cloud/test/cloud-microvm.test.ts
- test/cloud-benchmarks.test.ts -> browse_cloud/test/cloud-benchmarks.test.ts

Do NOT move test/cloud-sdk.test.ts -- it goes to browse_sdk (TASK-002).

**Import rewrites in cloud.test.ts (the critical boundary test):**
- `from '../src/cloud/auth'` -> `from '../src/auth'` (cloud-internal)
- `from '../src/cloud/sessions'` -> `from '../src/sessions'` (cloud-internal)
- `from '../src/session/manager'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'` (SessionManager)
- `from '../src/session/target-factory'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'` (createBrowserTargetFactory)
- `from '../src/session/persist'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'` (freezeSession, hasFrozenManifest, loadFrozenManifest, FrozenSessionManifest)
- `from '../src/commands/write'` -> `from '@ulpi/browse'` (handleWriteCommand)
- `from './test-server'` -> create browse_cloud/test/test-helpers.ts with a minimal HTTP test server

**Import rewrites in cloud-containers.test.ts, cloud-microvm.test.ts:**
- `from '../src/cloud/X'` -> `from '../src/X'` (cloud-internal only, no @ulpi/browse imports)

**cloud-benchmarks.test.ts:** No browse_cli imports, just move.

**Add vitest.config.ts** to browse_cloud.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` passes covering all test files. `npx vitest run test/cloud-benchmarks.test.ts` passes (pure unit tests). cloud.test.ts compiles against @ulpi/browse package -- this proves the boundary works.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 test files exist in browse_cloud/test/ with all imports updated. No references to ../src/cloud/ or ../src/session/ or ../src/commands/. Failure case: one import still uses relative path to browse_cli internals.

'npx tsc --noEmit' passes in browse_cloud/ covering ALL test files -- cloud.test.ts compiles against @ulpi/browse package imports. Failure case: handleWriteCommand not exported from lib.ts, tsc fails on cloud.test.ts.

'npx vitest run' in browse_cloud/ passes at least cloud-benchmarks.test.ts AND cloud-containers.test.ts (both are module-level, no browser needed). cloud.test.ts is expected to skip if no browser available but MUST compile. Failure case: @ulpi/browse runtime import fails at test startup because dist/lib.mjs is stale.

tasks.title: Remove src/cloud/ and src/sdk/ from browse_cli
tasks.description:
Clean removal of extracted code from browse_cli. This must be done AFTER browse_cloud and browse_sdk are scaffolded and verified.

1. **Delete directories:**
   - src/cloud/ (15 files)
   - src/sdk/ (6 files, 1 subdirectory)

2. **Delete test files:**
   - test/cloud.test.ts
   - test/cloud-containers.test.ts
   - test/cloud-microvm.test.ts
   - test/cloud-benchmarks.test.ts
   - test/cloud-sdk.test.ts

3. **Remove package.json 'cloud' script:** Delete `"cloud": "tsx src/cloud/server.ts"` from scripts.

4. **Verify no remaining references:**
   - grep -r 'cloud/' src/ returns zero matches (excluding comments)
   - grep -r 'sdk/' src/ returns zero matches (excluding sdk.ts which stays)
   - src/sdk.ts (local SDK) must NOT be deleted -- it stays in browse_cli

5. **Verify builds pass:**
   - npm run build succeeds (both CLI and lib targets)
   - npx tsc --noEmit passes
   - npm test passes (remaining tests, no cloud test failures)
acceptanceCriteria: src/cloud/ and src/sdk/ directories do not exist in browse_cli; src/sdk.ts still exists and compiles. Failure case: src/sdk.ts accidentally deleted because it was confused with src/sdk/ directory.
'npm run build' succeeds and 'npm test' passes with no cloud-related test failures. Failure case: test runner still discovers test/cloud*.test.ts because files weren't deleted.

'grep -rn "from.*[./]cloud/" src/' and 'grep -rn "from.*[./]sdk/" src/' return zero matches -- no dangling imports. Failure case: src/lib.ts still imports from session/concurrency which imports from cloud/.

tasks.title: Fix direct-mode freeze: use actual session domainFilter
acceptanceCriteria: Direct-mode freeze reads the actual session's domainFilter.domains and persists allowedDomains in the frozen manifest -- not hardcoded null. Failure case: session.domainFilter is accessed but .domains property doesn't exist on the DomainFilter class.
Freeze with a domain-restricted session produces a manifest file where allowedDomains is a non-empty array. Failure case: domainFilter exists but domains getter returns undefined.

tasks.title: Fix direct-mode resume: call loadSessionState/resumeSession to restore tabs/cookies/state
tasks.description:
Fix BLOCK 1 in browse_cloud/src/server.ts.

**Bug:** Line ~474-484 provisions a new session but never calls resumeSession() to restore tabs, cookies, localStorage, or active tab:
```typescript
const result = await cloudSessions.provision(tenant.tenantId, { sessionId });
// BUG: session is blank -- user's page state is lost
```

**Fix:** After provisioning the new session, call resumeSession() or the equivalent restore flow:
1. Load the frozen manifest via loadFrozenManifest()
2. Use the session's target to restore cookies/localStorage via loadSessionState()
3. Navigate to saved tab URLs
4. Switch to the previously active tab
5. If allowedDomains exist in the manifest, re-apply DomainFilter to the new session

The resumeSession() function in session/persist.ts handles steps 1-4 with graceful fallbacks. The domainFilter re-application must be done separately since it's cloud-specific (the session was provisioned by CloudSessionManager, not by resumeSession's factory).
acceptanceCriteria: Direct-mode resume restores tabs, cookies, localStorage, and active tab from the frozen manifest -- a round-trip freeze->resume preserves the user's page state. Failure case: resumeSession creates a new target instead of using the already-provisioned session's target.

Resume with allowedDomains in manifest re-applies the domain filter to the new session. Failure case: domain filter is read from manifest but never applied because the DomainFilter constructor path differs from SessionManager.getOrCreate().
Resume when manifest is missing or corrupt returns a clear error, not a crash. Failure case: loadFrozenManifest throws on missing file and error is unhandled.

tasks.title: Fix VM resume handle: set allowedDomains from frozen metadata + guard VM mode without networkProvider
acceptanceCriteria: Resumed VmSessionHandle has allowedDomains copied from frozen.allowedDomains. Failure case: FrozenSession interface in orchestrator-interface.ts doesn't have an allowedDomains field.

VmOrchestrator constructor THROWS (not warns) when no networkProvider is configured. Failure case: existing code that constructs VmOrchestrator without networkProvider now crashes at startup instead of at provision time -- caller must be updated to handle this or not construct without provider.

Cloud server with KVM available but no networkProvider logs 'VmOrchestrator init failed' and falls back to direct mode (orchestrator remains null). Failure case: server.ts try/catch missing — process exits instead of falling back.

tasks.title: Wire per-key maxConcurrent enforcement in cloud server
tasks.description:
CONCERN 5: JWT carries maxConcurrent per API key but cloud server never enforces it.

1. Export ConcurrencyLimiter, ConcurrencyLimitError, withUserLimit from src/lib.ts (they're in session/concurrency.ts).

2. In browse_cloud/src/server.ts, create a module-scope ConcurrencyLimiter.

3. Wrap handleSessionCommand dispatch with per-tenant concurrency limiting via withUserLimit().

4. Return HTTP 429 when limit exceeded with clear message including the limit value.

5. Default to 6 when JWT has no maxConcurrent (use CLOUD_DEFAULTS or hardcoded default).

Note: This task also requires adding ConcurrencyLimiter, ConcurrencyLimitError, and withUserLimit to src/lib.ts exports in browse_cli. Coordinate with TASK-001 or add them as a follow-up edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-key maxConcurrent limits concurrent command execution per tenant -- sending maxConcurrent+1 simultaneous commands returns HTTP 429 for the excess. Failure case: withUserLimit is not awaited, so the limit check is skipped.
HTTP 429 response includes a clear message with the limit value and guidance to retry. Failure case: ConcurrencyLimitError is thrown but not caught, resulting in HTTP 500.
When JWT does not specify maxConcurrent, global default (6) applies. Failure case: undefined maxConcurrent passes to withUserLimit as NaN, causing all commands to be rejected.

tasks.title: Document WS streaming limitation + clean up VM test placeholders
tasks.description:
Two cleanup items.

**WS streaming (CONCERN 6 -- out of scope):** Add JSDoc at server.ts WS upgrade handler (~line 637) documenting:
- Why WS streaming doesn't work for isolated sessions (would require WS proxy to container internal server)
- What would be needed to implement it
- That this is a known limitation, not a bug
Also ensure the WS handler returns a proper error message when a client tries to connect to an isolated session.

**VM test placeholders (CONCERN 7):** Two tests in cloud-microvm.test.ts (lines ~641, ~657) are placeholders that only check fc.checkKvm(). Convert to test.todo() with descriptive messages OR implement mock-based tests. Remove fake placeholder implementations.

**VM networking (BLOCK 3 -- out of scope):** Add a comment in vm-orchestrator.ts near the networkProvider check documenting that VM networking requires host-side tap/NAT/DHCP setup and is not solvable in application code.
acceptanceCriteria: WS streaming limitation documented with clear JSDoc in server.ts -- grep for 'WS streaming' or 'isolated session' returns the documentation. Failure case: comment added but not at the right location (must be at the WS upgrade handler, not a random location).

VM lifecycle tests in cloud-microvm.test.ts are either test.todo() with descriptive messages or real mock-based tests -- no more placeholder tests that just check KVM availability. Failure case: tests converted to test.todo but descriptions are vague ('TODO: implement').

tasks.title: Final verification -- all 3 repos compile, browse_cloud tests run, browse_cli tests pass
tasks.description:
End-to-end verification that all three repositories work independently.

1. **browse_cli** (post-extraction):
   - `npm run build` succeeds (dist/browse.cjs + dist/lib.mjs + dist/types/lib.d.ts)
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
   - `npm test` passes (all remaining tests, no cloud/sdk test references)
   - `node dist/browse.cjs --help` shows help text
   - No imports in src/ reference cloud/ or sdk/ directories
   - src/sdk.ts (local SDK) still exists and compiles

2. **browse_sdk** (extracted SDK):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
   - `npx vitest run` passes (sdk.test.ts)
   - grep confirms zero @ulpi/browse dependencies

3. **browse_cloud** (extracted cloud):
   - `npm install` succeeds (pulls @ulpi/browse via file:../browse_cli)
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
   - `npx vitest run` passes both cloud-benchmarks.test.ts AND cloud-containers.test.ts (proves tests compile and run against @ulpi/browse)
   - All imports use @ulpi/browse package (no relative ../session/ etc.)

4. **Cross-repo guard:**
   - grep -r 'browse_cloud' browse_cli/src/ returns zero matches
   - grep -r 'browse_cli' browse_cloud/src/ returns zero matches (only package.json file: link)
   - grep -r '@ulpi/browse' browse_sdk/src/ returns zero matches
acceptanceCriteria: All three repos compile independently: 'npx tsc --noEmit' passes in each of browse_cli, browse_sdk, browse_cloud. Failure case: browse_cloud tsc fails because a type was removed from lib.ts by a bug fix task.

browse_cli 'npm run build' produces dist/browse.cjs + dist/lib.mjs + dist/types/lib.d.ts, and 'npm test' passes with no cloud/sdk test references. Failure case: vitest still discovers deleted test/cloud*.test.ts via glob pattern.

browse_cloud 'npx vitest run' passes both cloud-benchmarks.test.ts AND cloud-containers.test.ts (module-level, no browser needed). This proves the boundary works end-to-end with real test execution, not just compilation. Failure case: vitest runs but all tests skip because of missing infra.

layers.notes: Independent foundation: library build target + types pipeline (TASK-001) and SDK scaffold (TASK-002)
Cloud scaffold depends on library exports from TASK-001
Cloud boundary tests + bug fixes, all depend on TASK-003
Remove from CLI (after tests moved) + documentation/test cleanup
Final cross-repo verification including test validation

executionSummary.shipCut: Minimum shippable: TASK-001 + TASK-002 + TASK-003 + TASK-004 + TASK-005 = clean extraction with working builds AND test validation proving the boundary works. Bug fixes (TASK-006 through TASK-010) can ship as follow-up.
